rationale: Verified at pinned 15dab51b (worktree clean throughout, all scratch under /tmp). Finding (a) ADDRESSED: TestSanitizeNonBeadDecision now has 9 rows incl. leg (3) abandoned and leg (4) round-mismatch, both built from real panel.PanelGateDecision (not hand-constructed messages) via buildResult/basePanel with Abandoned=true and Round mismatch respectively. `go test ./cmd/mindspec -run 'Sanitize|NonBead' -v` green, all 9 sub-tests pass. Finding (b) refutation ACCEPTED: empirically ran the built binary in a /tmp scratch repo, `mindspec panel create demo --spec s --target ""` -> `Error: --target is required`, exit 1, no panel.json written -- confirms panel.go:100 `strings.TrimSpace(target) == ""` guard makes an empty Target genuinely CLI-unreachable, so the double-space cosmetic artifact only exists via hand-corrupted panel.json, outside the finding's own severity claim. Full suite: `go test ./cmd/mindspec` ok (15.7s), `go build ./...` clean, `git show --name-only HEAD` = exactly the 3 declared files, `git diff b641a9fe 15dab51b -- cmd/mindspec/panel.go` empty (byte-identical to round 1). git status clean.

findings.description: Round-1 finding (a) ADDRESSED: TestSanitizeNonBeadDecision now covers legs (2),(3),(4),(5),(5b),(6),(8),(9),(10), including the previously-missing leg (4) round-mismatch row and an added leg (3) abandoned row, both constructed via real panel.PanelGateDecision(facts) rather than synthetic messages. Verified green via -run 'Sanitize|NonBead' -v.

Round-1 finding (b) REFUTED by implementer and ACCEPTED on independent empirical verification: `panel create` rejects an empty --target with `--target is required` (panel.go ~line 100, strings.TrimSpace(target)=="") before any panel.json is written, so a non-bead panel.json with Target=="" cannot occur via the CLI. The double-space artifact in sanitizeNonBeadDecision's leg (5b) message is confirmed CLI-unreachable and remains a non-blocking cosmetic note applicable only to hand-crafted malformed fixtures.
